9.display: Yes — at x = 2, y = 2 + 3 = 5, which matches.

10.display: Yes — at x = 4, y = 2(4) + 1 = 9, which matches.

13.display: Yes — every point on y = 3 has a y-value of 3 no matter what x is, so (7, 3) is on the line.

14.display: Always — setting x = 0 in y = x + c gives y = c, so the line always crosses the y-axis at (0, c).

15.display: At x = 3, y = 3(3) + 1 = 10, which matches the point (3, 10), so it is on the line.

16.display: They cross at (4, 4) — every point on x = 4 has x-value 4, and every point on y = 4 has y-value 4, so the only point satisfying both is (4, 4).

17.display: True — for y = 2x, the y-value is always exactly twice the x-value by definition of the equation.

18.display: Leo mixed up the axis crossings — (0, -5) is where the line crosses the y-axis. The x-axis crossing is where y = 0: 0 = x - 5, so x = 5, giving the point (5, 0).

19.display: No — y = x + 1 crosses the y-axis at (0, 1), while y = x - 1 crosses at (0, -1). They are 2 units apart.

20.display: The equation is y = x, since in every point the y-value equals the x-value: 2 = 2, 4 = 4, 6 = 6.

21.display: (2, 2) — the pattern increases by 0.5 each time, so at time 2, y = 1 + 2(0.5) = 2.

22.display: The delivery charge is a fixed £5 no matter how many items are ordered — it does not change with x.

23.display: It is vertical because the fee is fixed at x = 2 regardless of the value plotted on the y-axis — the line has a constant x-value rather than a constant rate of change.

24.display: The line crosses the y-axis at (0, 20), meaning the starting cost before any months have passed is £20 (e.g. a fixed joining fee).

25.display: Both give x = 3 when y = 9: 2(3) + 3 = 9 and 3(3) = 9.

26.display: Midpoint is (2, 8). Checking y = 2x + 4 at x = 2 gives y = 8, so the midpoint does lie on that line.

27.display: Gradient = (8 - 4)/(3 - 1) = 2, so the equation is y = 2x + 2. Example third point: (5, 12), since 2(5) + 2 = 12.

30.display: At x = 1, y = 2(1) + 1 = 3. At x = 2, y = 2(2) + 1 = 5. At x = 4, y = 2(4) + 1 = 9. All three match, confirming the points lie on the line.
